text: The surgeon performs laparoscopic salvage lateral pelvic lymph node dissection, beginning with careful dissection of small intestine adhesions in the pelvic cavity. The right ureter is isolated, dissected, and displaced toward the left side. Dissection continues to the vesicoureteral junction, revealing fibrosis at the outer side of the pelvic plexus. The external iliac artery and vein are exposed.

The surgeon exposes the psoas major muscle and performs dissection along its surface. The internal obturator muscle is revealed, and a tiny vessel is divided using an ultrasonically activated scalpel. Dissection proceeds along the vesico-hypogastric fascia and the surface of the internal obturator muscle, with careful identification and preservation of the obturator nerve.

The surgeon exposes the sacral plexus and divides the distal and proximal sides of the obturator vessels. The proximal and distal sides of the umbilical artery are also divided, followed by the division of the distal side of the inferior vesicle artery.

The surgeon exposes the levator ani muscle and the main trunk of the internal iliac artery. The proximal side of the superior vesicle artery is divided, followed by the division of the proximal side of the internal iliac artery. The surface of the bladder is exposed, and the distal side of the inferior vesicle vein is divided, followed by its proximal side. The surface of the internal iliac vein is also exposed.

The surgeon divides the proximal side of another inferior vesicle vein and the distal side of the internal pudendal artery. The distal side of the inferior gluteal artery is also divided. The surface of the internal iliac vein is fully exposed. Tissue is dissected from the pelvic plexus, and the distal side of the inferior vesicle artery is divided, followed by the distal side of another inferior vesicle artery.
The surgeon completes the dissection, revealing the final anatomical structures in the pelvic region, including the exposed external iliac artery and vein, and the displaced right ureter.
